pi_items.source: Calculated from task specifications: Shank cylinder (π×9²×20=5089.4mm³) + Fork head block (20×30×30=18000mm³) - Slot (20×12×30=7200mm³) - Pin hole (π×5²×30=2356.2mm³) = 13533.2mm³
Reasonable engineering tolerance; typical for mechanical parts ±10% variation
Calculated from specifications: Shank extends from X=-20 to X=0, Fork head and shank both span Y from -15 to +15, and Z from -15 to +15
Calculated from specifications: Fork head extends from X=0 to X=20, combined with shank Y/Z ranges of ±15mm
Task specification states: 'A 10mm diameter through-hole passing through both arms of the fork'
Task specification states: 'A 12mm wide gap centered on the Y-axis (removing material between Y=-6 and Y=6)'

privileged_info_summary: The task is based on DIN 71752 standard for clevis rod ends. The geometry consists of: (1) a cylindrical shank with diameter 18mm and length 20mm (X range: -20 to 0), (2) a rectangular fork head block 20×30×30mm (X: 0-20, Y: -15 to 15, Z: -15 to 15), (3) a rectangular slot 12mm wide at the center removing 7200mm³ of material, and (4) a 10mm diameter through-hole passing through the fork. The calculated volume based on these specifications is approximately 13,533 mm³, which contradicts the metadata claim of 14,475 mm³ (difference of 942 mm³ or 6.96%). The expected volume could only reach 14,475 mm³ if either the hole length was reduced to ~18mm (contradicting the 'through-hole' description) or if the slot width was narrowed to ~10.43mm (contradicting the stated 12mm slot width). The bounding box dimensions and other geometric specifications are consistent and verifiable.
